# sphinxcontrib-bibtex

{{ ci }} {{ codecov }} {{ version }} {{ license }}

Sphinx extension for BibTeX style citations.

## Overview

The bibtex extension allows [BibTeX](http://www.bibtex.org/)
citations to be inserted into documentation generated by
[Sphinx](https://www.sphinx-doc.org/en/master/), via
a `bibliography` directive,
along with `:cite:p:` and `:cite:t:` roles.
These work similarly to LaTeX's `thebibliography` environment
and the `\citet` and `\citep` commands.

For formatting, the extension relies on
[pybtex](https://pybtex.org/)
written by Andrey Golovizin.
The extension is inspired by Matthew Brett's
[bibstuff.sphinxext.bibref](https://github.com/matthew-brett/bibstuff)
and Weston Nielson's
[sphinx-natbib](https://github.com/mcmtroffaes/sphinxcontrib-bibtex/blob/develop/test/natbib.py).

- Download: <https://pypi.org/project/sphinxcontrib-bibtex/#files>
- Documentation: <https://sphinxcontrib-bibtex.readthedocs.io/en/latest/>
- Development: <https://github.com/mcmtroffaes/sphinxcontrib-bibtex/>

## Installation

Install the module with `pip install sphinxcontrib-bibtex`, or from
source using `pip install -e .`. Then add:

```python
extensions = ['sphinxcontrib.bibtex']
bibtex_bibfiles = ['refs.bib']
```

to your project's Sphinx configuration file `conf.py`.

Installation with `python setup.py install` is discouraged due to potential
issues with the sphinxcontrib namespace.

## Minimal Example

In your project's documentation, you can use
`:cite:t:` for textual citation references,
`:cite:p:` for parenthetical citation references,
and `.. bibliography::` for inserting the bibliography.
For [example](https://github.com/mcmtroffaes/sphinxcontrib-bibtex/tree/develop/test/roots/test-debug_minimal_example):

```rest
See :cite:t:`1987:nelson` for an introduction to non-standard analysis.
Non-standard analysis is fun :cite:p:`1987:nelson`.

.. bibliography::
```

where `refs.bib` would contain an entry:

```
@Book{1987:nelson,
  author = {Edward Nelson},
  title = {Radically Elementary Probability Theory},
  publisher = {Princeton University Press},
  year = {1987}
}
```

In the default style, this will get rendered as:

See Nelson [^cite_nel87a] for an introduction to non-standard analysis.
Non-standard analysis is fun [^cite_nel87a].

[^cite_nel87a]: Edward Nelson. *Radically Elementary Probability Theory*. Princeton University Press, 1987.

Citations in sphinx are resolved globally across all documents.
Typically, you have a single `bibliography` directive across
your entire project which collects all citations.
Advanced use cases with multiple `bibliography` directives
across your project are also supported, but some care
needs to be taken from your end to avoid duplicate citations.

In contrast, footnotes in sphinx are resolved locally per document.
To achieve local bibliographies per document, you can use citations
represented by footnotes as follows:

```rest
See :footcite:t:`1987:nelson` for an introduction to non-standard analysis.
Non-standard analysis is fun\ :footcite:p:`1987:nelson`.

.. footbibliography::
```

which will get rendered as:

See Nelson[^nel87b] for an introduction to non-standard analysis.
Non-standard analysis is fun[^nel87b].

[^nel87b]: Edward Nelson. *Radically Elementary Probability Theory*. Princeton University Press, 1987.

Note the use of the
[backslash escaped space](https://www.sphinx-doc.org/en/master/usage/restructuredtext/basics.html#inline-markup)
to suppress the space that would otherwise precede the footnote.

Typically, you have a single `footbibliography` directive
at the bottom of each document that has footnote citations.
Advanced use cases with multiple `footbibliography` directives
per document are also supported. Since everything is local,
there is no concern with duplicate citations when using footnotes.

functions.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.referencing.format_references
documentation:
Format the list of references according to the given role.

First formats each reference using the style's
{meth}`~BaseReferenceStyle.inner` method,
then joins all these formatted references together using
the style's {meth}`~BaseReferenceStyle.outer` method.

classes.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.referencing.BaseReferenceStyle

methods.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.referencing.BaseReferenceStyle.__post_init__
documentation: see https://stackoverflow.com/a/59987363 as to why this is here

methods.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.referencing.BaseReferenceStyle.role_names
documentation: Get list of role names supported by this style.

methods.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.referencing.BaseReferenceStyle.outer
documentation: Returns outer template for formatting the references.

methods.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.referencing.BaseReferenceStyle.inner
documentation: Returns inner template for formatting the references.

Base class for citation reference styles.

For consistency, all subclasses of this class must be decorated
as a {class}`dataclass`,
and must provide a type annotation and default value for all attributes
(unless `init=False` is used, in which case they can be
initialized in {meth}`~dataclass.__post_init__`).
This allows client code to instantiate any reference style
without needing to specify any arguments through the constructor.

and a function to facilitate formatting of the outer template.

classes.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.richtext.BaseReferenceText
documentation:
Generic rich text element for citation references.
Instances store some extra reference info that can be used when formatting.
This base class renders its children without further formatting.
Implementations must create a derivation from this class which
overrides the *render* method to create the desired output.
See for instance
{class}`~sphinxcontrib.bibtex.domain.SphinxReferenceText`.

classes.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.directives.BibliographyDirective

methods.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.directives.BibliographyDirective._get_filter
documentation: Get parsed filter from options.

methods.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.directives.BibliographyDirective.run
documentation:
Process .bib files, set file dependencies, and create a
node that is to be transformed to the entries of the
bibliography.

Class for processing the {rst:dir}`bibliography` directive.

Produces a
{class}`~sphinxcontrib.bibtex.nodes.bibliography` node,
along with (empty) citation nodes that will be formatted later in the
*env-updated* stage, and inserted into the document in a post-transform.
We cannot insert the citation nodes here because we do not yet know
which keys have been cited.

:::{seealso}
Further processing of the resulting
{class}`~sphinxcontrib.bibtex.nodes.bibliography` node is done
by
{class}`~sphinxcontrib.bibtex.transforms.BibliographyTransform`.
:::

Join text fragments, capitalize the first letter,
and add a period to the end.
copied from pybtex names but using the new join

copied from pybtex names but using the new join allowing "et al" formatting

functions.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.template.entry_label
documentation: Node for inserting the label of a formatted entry.

functions.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.template.reference
documentation:
Pybtex node for inserting a docutils reference node to a citation.
The children of the node
comprise the content of the reference, and any referencing information
is stored in the *reference_info* key of the *data*.
The data must also contain a *style* key pointing to the corresponding
{class}`~sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.referencing.BaseReferenceStyle`.

functions.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.template.footnote_reference
documentation:
Pybtex node for inserting a footnote_reference docutils node.
Any referencing information
is stored in the *reference_info* key of the *data*.
The data must also contain a *style* key pointing to the corresponding
{class}`~sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.referencing.BaseReferenceStyle`.

classes.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.template.SphinxReferenceInfo

class_variables.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.template.SphinxReferenceInfo.builder
documentation: : The Sphinx builder.

class_variables.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.template.SphinxReferenceInfo.fromdocname
documentation: : Document name of the citation reference.

class_variables.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.template.SphinxReferenceInfo.todocname
documentation: : Document name of the bibliography.

class_variables.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.template.SphinxReferenceInfo.citation_id
documentation: : Unique id of the citation within the bibliography.

class_variables.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.template.SphinxReferenceInfo.title
documentation: : Title attribute for reference node.

class_variables.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.template.SphinxReferenceInfo.pre_text
documentation: : Text to come before citation.

class_variables.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.template.SphinxReferenceInfo.post_text
documentation: : Text to come after citation.

Tuple containing reference info to enable sphinx to resolve a reference
to a citation.

classes.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.template.SphinxReferenceText
documentation:
Pybtex rich text class generating
a docutils reference node to a citation
for use with {class}`SphinxReferenceInfo`.

class_variables.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.template.FootReferenceInfo.document
documentation: : Current docutils document.

documentation: : Citation reference name.

Tuple containing reference info to enable sphinx to resolve a footnote
reference.

classes.name: sphinxcontrib.bibtex.style.template.FootReferenceText

Pybtex rich text class generating

for use with {class}`FootReferenceInfo`.
